thread.title: Join the fight against human trafficking at Signs of Hope's Night Out with News 3
thread.title_full: Join the fight against human trafficking at Signs of Hope's Night Out with News 3

author: News 3 Staff
title: Join the fight against human trafficking at Signs of Hope's Night Out with News 3
text: {p}{ }Flag for usage of AI Transcript Assistant{/p}{p}{ } Proof of concept AI assistant.{/p}{p}The 12th annual \"Hope for the Holidays: Night Out with News 3\" event, hosted by Signs of Hope, is set to take place on Wednesday, December 11, at TAO in The Venetian. {/p}{p}Attendees can enjoy cocktails, a wine pull, and a silent auction.{/p}{p}Proceeds from the event will support thousands of survivors of sexual violence and human trafficking, providing them with hope and healing. {/p}{p}Tickets are available at{a href=\"https://sohlv.org/\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://sohlv.org/\"} www.sohlv.org{/a}.{/p}{p}HEADLINE: Another chilly night ahead{/p}{p}LOW TODAY: 36{ } { } { }NORMAL LOW: 40{/p}{p}HIGH TODAY: 54{ } { } { } { } { }NORMAL HIGH: 57{/p}{p}It was a chilly day from start to finish as highs today around the valley remained in the mid 50s under sunny skies.{/p}{p}Here are some Tuesday morning lows from across the valley:{/p}{p}Henderson Executive: 28; Nellis: 36; North Las Vegas Airport: 36; Aliante: 32; Summerlin: 37; Mountain's Edge: 34; Southern Highlands: 32; Centennial: 38; Anthem: 35{/p}{p}Expect another chilly one tonight as lows drop into the mid and upper 30s under partly cloudy skies.{/p}{p}Scattered clouds will arrive tomorrow ahead of a storm system that will arrive Thursday.{/p}{p}These clouds will help lock temperatures in the mid 50s once again on Wednesday.{/p}{p}Winds will ramp up on Thursday ahead of the cold front. These winds will push temperatures close to the 60 mark before highs drop back down into the 50s on Friday.{/p}{p}Precipitation chances on Thursday remain low for the valley, but an isolated rain or snow shower is possible in our local mountains.{/p}{p}By midnight tonight, Las Vegas will reach 150 consecutive days since our last measurable rainfall.{/p}{p}The 8 to 14 day outlook calls for warmer and drier than normal conditions. It may not be until the 3rd week of December until we see a pattern shift.{/p}{p}Severe to extreme drought conditions have developed across southern Nevada, including the Las Vegas Valley.{/p}{p}SNOWPACK - UPPER COLORADO: RIGHT NOW: 91% A YEAR AGO: 77%{/p}{p}Lake Mead is currently at 1061 feet compared to 1065 feet one year ago today.{/p}{p}Total valley rainfall in 2024: 2.27\" (-1.56\" below normal){/p}{p}Total valley rainfall through December 10th, 2023: 4.53\" (+0.70\" above normal){/p}{p}AIR QUALITY - HEALTHY{/p}{p}POLLEN COUNT:{/p}{p}TREES: LOW{/p}{p}GRASS: LOW{/p}{p}MOLD: LOW{/p}{p}WEEDS: LOW{/p}{p}-Bill Bellis{/p}{p}{ }Flag for usage of AI Transcript Assistant{/p}{p}{ } Proof of concept AI assistant.{/p}{p}The 12th annual \"Hope for the Holidays: Night Out with News 3\" event, hosted by Signs of Hope, is set to take place on Wednesday, December 11, at TAO in The Venetian. {/p}{p}Attendees can enjoy cocktails, a wine pull, and a silent auction.{/p}{p}Proceeds from the event will support thousands of survivors of sexual violence and human trafficking, providing them with hope and healing. {/p}{p}Tickets are available at{a href=\"https://sohlv.org/\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://sohlv.org/\"} www.sohlv.org{/a}.{/p}{p}HEADLINE: Another chilly night ahead{/p}{p}LOW TODAY: 36{ } { } { }NORMAL LOW: 40{/p}{p}HIGH TODAY: 54{ } { } { } { } { }NORMAL HIGH: 57{/p}{p}It was a chilly day from start to finish as highs today around the valley remained in the mid 50s under sunny skies.{/p}{p}Here are some Tuesday morning lows from across the valley:{/p}{p}Henderson Executive: 28; Nellis: 36; North Las Vegas Airport: 36; Aliante: 32; Summerlin: 37; Mountain's Edge: 34; Southern Highlands: 32; Centennial: 38; Anthem: 35{/p}{p}Expect another chilly one tonight as lows drop into the mid and upper 30s under partly cloudy skies.{/p}{p}Scattered clouds will arrive tomorrow ahead of a storm system that will arrive Thursday.{/p}{p}These clouds will help lock temperatures in the mid 50s once again on Wednesday.{/p}{p}Winds will ramp up on Thursday ahead of the cold front. These winds will push temperatures close to the 60 mark before highs drop back down into the 50s on Friday.{/p}{p}Precipitation chances on Thursday remain low for the valley, but an isolated rain or snow shower is possible in our local mountains.{/p}{p}By midnight tonight, Las Vegas will reach 150 consecutive days since our last measurable rainfall.{/p}{p}The 8 to 14 day outlook calls for warmer and drier than normal conditions. It may not be until the 3rd week of December until we see a pattern shift.{/p}{p}Severe to extreme drought conditions have developed across southern Nevada, including the Las Vegas Valley.{/p}{p}SNOWPACK - UPPER COLORADO: RIGHT NOW: 91% A YEAR AGO: 77%{/p}{p}Lake Mead is currently at 1061 feet compared to 1065 feet one year ago today.{/p}{p}Total valley rainfall in 2024: 2.27\" (-1.56\" below normal){/p}{p}Total valley rainfall through December 10th, 2023: 4.53\" (+0.70\" above normal){/p}{p}AIR QUALITY - HEALTHY{/p}{p}POLLEN COUNT:{/p}{p}TREES: LOW{/p}{p}GRASS: LOW{/p}{p}MOLD: LOW{/p}{p}WEEDS: LOW{/p}{p}-Bill Bellis{/p}
